summary: A symmetrical fortress-style structure with four tall corner towers topped with overhanging wooden platforms. The central body features horizontal striped walls in stone and wood, with a recessed interior courtyard visible from above. The design emphasizes defensive architecture with prominent corner bastions and a flat roof system.
rebuild_hints: Build four corner towers first, each approximately 2x2 blocks wide and 12 blocks tall
Create overhanging wooden platforms at tower tops using stairs or slabs extending 3-4 blocks outward
Construct central rectangular walls with alternating stonebrick and wood horizontal stripes
Space towers at corners with approximately 10-12 block gaps between centers
Add small window openings (1-2 blocks) scattered across tower faces
Place fence blocks around the entire perimeter base
Create flat roof using stone slabs across the top
Interior courtyard appears to be open ground level with decorative pattern
uncertainties: Exact interior courtyard layout and decoration pattern unclear from exterior views
Precise window placement and size varies by tower face
Exact height of corner towers relative to central structure uncertain; estimate based on visual proportion
Material composition of horizontal stripes may include variants of stone or wood blocks
Roof overhang depth and exact construction method not fully visible
